html:
[1211]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 象牙筠箪碧纱笼（栊） - Compound phrase describing an ornate object: a bamboo basket lined with ivory and wrapped in fine green silk.
  • 绰约佳人 - Classical phrase meaning 'graceful and delicate beauty,' often used to describe a woman of refined and elegant appearance.
  • 半抹晓烟笼芍药 - Poetic image of morning mist partially covering peony flowers, creating a soft, dreamlike scene.
  • 一泓秋水浸芙蓉 - Metaphor for the beauty of a woman’s eyes, comparing them to a pool of autumn water reflecting lotus flowers.
  • 神游蓬岛三千界 - Refers to a spiritual journey to the mythical island of Penglai, associated with immortality in Chinese mythology.
  • 梦绕巫山十二峰 - Reference to the Twelve Peaks of Wushan, a legendary mountain range in Chinese folklore.
  • 谁把棋声惊觉后 - Poetic question asking who disturbed the dream with the sound of a game of Go, causing the woman to wake.
  • 起来香汗湿酥胸 - Vivid image of the woman waking, her body drenched in fragrant sweat, emphasizing her physical beauty and sensuality.
  • 怒其轻薄 - To be angry at someone for being frivolous or lacking respect.
  • 深居不出 - To remain secluded and not appear in public.
  • 悒悒而归 - To return home in a state of sorrow or dejection.
  • 托邻妪投之诗曰 - To entrust an elderly neighbor woman to deliver a poem.
  • 桂殿焚香酒半醒 - Scene in a fragrant hall where someone is half-awake after drinking wine, perhaps in a dream or reverie.
  • 露华如水点银屏 - Poetic image of dew like water dotting a silver screen, suggesting a scene of quiet beauty.
  • 含情欲诉心中事 - To hold feelings and want to express something in the heart.
  • 羞见牵牛织女星 - To feel shy to look at the stars of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl, suggesting a romantic or emotional hesitation.
  • 梨花寂寂斗婵娟 - Metaphor comparing the quiet beauty of pear blossoms to the grace of a beautiful woman.
  • 银汉斜临绣户前 - The Milky Way is tilted over the embroidered window, suggesting a romantic or poetic setting.
  • 自爱焚香消永夜 - To enjoy the act of burning incense to pass the long night.
  • 从来无事诉青天 - To have never had anything to say to the heavens, suggesting a sense of solitude or unspoken thoughts.
  • 云娥酷似董娇娆 - A woman is very similar to Dong Jiao Rao, a legendary beauty from ancient Chinese literature.
  • 谁道蓬山天样远 - Who would say that Penglai is as far as heaven, suggesting that even distant places are close in the heart.
  • 画阑咫尺是红桥 - The painted railing is just a step away from the red bridge, a metaphor for closeness or romance.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 象牙筠箪碧纱笼（栊） - This compound object may be a luxurious scroll case or a decorative item, symbolizing refinement and cultural sophistication.
  • 绰约佳人 - Classical poetic term for a graceful and beautiful woman, often used in romantic or elegiac poetry.
  • 半抹晓烟笼芍药，一泓秋水浸芙蓉 - Lines use natural imagery (peony, lotus) to describe a woman’s beauty, a common technique in classical Chinese poetry.
  • 蓬岛 - Penglai, one of the three mythical islands of immortality in Chinese mythology.
  • 巫山十二峰 - The Twelve Peaks of Wushan, a legendary mountain range in Sichuan, famous for its beauty and its association with the myth of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl.
  • 棋声 - The sound of a game of Go (weiqi), a traditional Chinese board game, often symbolizing leisure, intellect, or a quiet moment.
  • 香汗湿酥胸 - Poetic and sensual image, common in classical Chinese poetry, which uses scent, sweat, and softness to evoke romantic or erotic themes.
  • 张得之，怒其轻薄 - The name 'Zhang Dezhi' is a proper noun; the action of being angry at someone for being 'light and frivolous' suggests a moral or social judgment.
  • 偁悒悒而归 - The name 'Cheng' is a proper noun; the term 'yì yì' means sorrowful or dejected, indicating emotional distress.
  • 桂殿 - A hall or palace decorated with osmanthus, often associated with the moon or the celestial realm in poetry.
  • 牵牛织女星 - The mythological figures of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl, separated by the Milky Way, a common romantic symbol in Chinese poetry.
  • 董娇娆 - A legendary beauty from ancient Chinese literature, often used as a standard of feminine beauty.
  • 蓬山 - Another name for Penglai, the mythical island of immortality.
  • 画阑咫尺是红桥 - A poetic image of a close, romantic space, possibly alluding to a bridge or a symbolic passage between lovers.

Proper Nouns

  • 张得之 - Zhang Dezhi, a proper noun, likely the name of the woman in the story.
  • 偁 - Cheng, a proper noun, likely the name of a man who is the subject of the story.
  • 林鸿 - Lin Hong, a proper noun, likely the name of Cheng’s friend.
  • 蓬岛 - Penglai, a mythical island of immortality.
  • 巫山十二峰 - The Twelve Peaks of Wushan, a legendary mountain range.
  • 牵牛织女星 - The mythological figures of the Cowherd and the Weaver Girl.
  • 董娇娆 - Dong Jiao Rao, a legendary beauty from ancient Chinese literature.

[1212]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 芙蓉作帐锦重重 - Poetic description of a luxurious bed or room adorned with lotus-patterned silks, symbolizing wealth and beauty.
  • 比翼和鸣玉漏中 - Refers to a pair of birds flying together and singing in harmony, often used as a metaphor for a harmonious couple, with '玉漏中' indicating the passage of time (a timepiece).
  • 共道瑶池春似海 - Metaphor comparing the springtime of the immortal realm (瑶池) to a vast sea, symbolizing boundless joy and abundance.
  • 月明飞下一双鸿 - Describes a pair of geese flying down under the bright moonlight, symbolizing the arrival of a couple or a moment of romantic fulfillment.
  • 卿独不闻庞公之妻，拜司马德操乎 - Reference to a historical anecdote where Pang De's wife shows respect to her husband's friend, used here to question whether the other party has heard of such an example.
  • 以吾之不可，学柳下惠之可 - Reference to the story of Zilu, who chose not to follow the example of Master Laozi, used here to express the idea of rejecting a certain behavior.
  • 酥 - Term for a soft, fatty substance, often used metaphorically to describe something delicate or smooth.
  • 香鬟三尺绾芙蓉 - Poetic description of a woman's hair adorned with lotus flowers, symbolizing beauty and elegance.
  • 翠耸巫山雨后峰 - Metaphor comparing the woman's hair to the green peaks of Mount Wushan after rain, symbolizing natural beauty.
  • 鸦翎蝉翼半蓬松 - Poetic description of the woman's hair, suggesting a slightly disheveled yet attractive appearance.
  • 溶溶春水漾璚瑶 - Metaphor comparing the spring water to a smooth, shimmering jade surface, symbolizing tranquility and beauty.
  • 几度踏青归去晚 - Refers to the act of going out for a spring outing and returning late, often associated with romantic or leisurely activities.
  • 灯火认红桥 - Refers to recognizing a red bridge by the light of lanterns, symbolizing a moment of recognition or reunion.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 芙蓉 - The lotus flower, a symbol of purity and beauty in Chinese culture.
  • 瑶池 - The legendary pool of immortals in Chinese mythology, often associated with eternal youth and abundance.
  • 庞公之妻，拜司马德操 - Historical anecdote from the *Book of Han* where Pang De's wife shows respect to her husband's friend, used here to illustrate a point about propriety.
  • 柳下惠 - Historical figure known for his moral integrity and refusal to compromise his principles, used here as a negative example.
  • 酥 - Term for a soft, fatty substance, often used metaphorically in poetry to describe something delicate or smooth.
  • 巫山 - A mountain range in China, often associated with romantic and poetic imagery.
  • 鸦翎蝉翼 - Poetic description of hair, suggesting a slightly disheveled yet attractive appearance.
  • 红桥 - A red bridge, often used in poetry to symbolize a moment of recognition or reunion.

Proper Nouns

  • 庞公 - Pang Gong, a historical figure from the *Book of Han*.
  • 司马德操 - Sima Decao, a historical figure from the *Book of Han*.
  • 柳下惠 - Zilu, a historical figure known for his moral integrity.
  • 巫山 - A mountain range in China.
  • 红桥 - A red bridge, often used in poetry.

[1213]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 素馨花發暗香飄 - Poetic description of a flower (likely *Jasminum sambac*) emitting a subtle fragrance; evokes a quiet, elegant scene.
  • 一朵斜簪近翠翘 - A woman wearing a single flower in her hair, close to her '翠翘' (ornamental hairpin made of jade or green material); combines beauty and modesty.
  • 宝马来归新月上 - A man or lover returns on a fine horse at the time of the new moon; suggests a romantic or nostalgic return.
  • 绿杨影里倚红桥 - Scene set in the shadow of willows near a red bridge; common poetic image for romantic or melancholic settings.
  • 玉阶凉露滴芭蕉 - '玉阶' (jade steps) suggests a refined setting; '凉露滴芭蕉' (cold dew dripping on banana plant) evokes a quiet, cool atmosphere.
  • 独倚屏山望斗杓 - Speaker alone, leaning against a screen or mountain, gazing at the '斗杓' (handle of the Big Dipper); expresses loneliness or contemplation.
  • 为惜碧波明月色 - Speaker's appreciation of moonlight reflecting on water ('碧波').
  • 凤头鞋子步红桥 - '凤头鞋子' refers to ornate shoes with a phoenix-shaped toe; describes speaker walking across a red bridge.
  • 桂轮斜落粉楼空 - '桂轮' is a poetic name for the moon; '粉楼空' (empty pink tower) evokes a desolate or abandoned setting.
  • 漏水丁丁烛影红 - '漏水丁丁' describes the sound of a water clock; '烛影红' (red candlelight) creates a warm, quiet atmosphere.
  • 露湿暗香珠翠冷 - Cold dew has dampened the fragrance of flowers; jewelry feels cold, suggesting a transition from warmth to chill.
  • 赤阑桥上待归鸿 - Speaker waiting on a red-railing bridge for the return of a wild goose; metaphor for waiting for a loved one.
  • 桥畔千花照碧空 - Thousands of flowers by the bridge reflect in the blue sky; evokes a vivid, colorful image.
  • 美人遥隔水云东 - A beautiful woman is far away, separated by water and clouds; expresses distance or longing.
  • 一声宝马嘶明月 - Neigh of a horse under the moon; adds a dramatic or emotional touch to the scene.
  • 惊起沙汀几点鸿 - Sound of the horse startles a few wild geese on a sandbar; common image in classical poetry to evoke movement and emotion.
  • 草香花暖醉春风 - Fragrance of grass and flowers intoxicates the speaker with the spring breeze; romantic and sensory description.
  • 郎去西湖水向东 - Beloved has gone; water of West Lake flows eastward; symbolizes passage of time or direction of the beloved.
  • 斜倚石阑频怅望 - Speaker leans against a stone railing and gazes out frequently with longing.
  • 月明孤影笑飞鸿 - Speaker alone under the bright moon, laughing at the flying geese; complex emotional state of solitude and melancholy.
  • 钟情太甚，人笑我，到老也无休歇 - Speaker is overly devoted to love; others laugh at them for continuing this passion even into old age.
  • 月露烟云多是恨，况与玉人离别 - Moon, dew, mist, and clouds are all sources of sorrow; especially in the context of parting with a beloved.
  • 软语叮咛，柔情婉恋（娈） - Gentle words and tender affection; '娈' possibly a variant of '恋' or '恋娈' as a poetic form of 'loving'.
  • 熔尽肝肠铁 - Speaker's heart and intestines have been melted into iron; metaphor for intensity of emotion.
  • 歧亭把酒，水流花谢时节 - Speaker drinks wine at a roadside pavilion during a time when the water flows and the flowers wither; metaphor for passage of time and sorrow of parting.
  • 应念翠袖笼香，玉壶温酒，夜夜银屏月 - Beloved should remember the speaker's presence in the form of fragrant sleeves, warm wine in a jade vessel, and moonlight on a silver screen.
  • 蓄喜含嗔多少态，海岳誓盟都设 - Beloved displays a range of emotions; speaker has made grand vows as vast as the sea and mountains.
  • 此去何之，碧云春树，合晚翠千叠 - Speaker questions where the beloved is going; image of green clouds and spring trees in the evening; suggests a distant, beautiful, and possibly unreachable place.
  • 图将羁思，归来细与伊说 - Speaker plans to record their longing and share it in detail with the beloved upon return.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 素馨花 - A type of jasmine, often used in poetry to symbolize purity and fragrance.
  • 翠翘 - A type of ornamental hairpin, often made of jade or green materials; used in classical Chinese poetry to describe a woman's elegance.
  • 宝马 - A fine horse; often used in poetry to symbolize nobility or a lover's return.
  • 红桥 - A red bridge; common poetic image symbolizing romantic or melancholic settings.
  • 玉阶 - Jade steps; used to describe elegant or refined settings, often in palace or garden contexts.
  • 屏山 - A screen or mountain; used in poetry to describe a place of solitude or contemplation.
  • 斗杓 - The handle of the Big Dipper; a star symbol used in poetry to represent time, direction, or the heavens.
  • 凤头鞋子 - A type of ornate shoe with a phoenix-shaped toe; used in poetry to describe elegance and refinement.
  • 桂轮 - A poetic name for the moon; often used in classical Chinese poetry.
  • 粉楼 - A pink tower or building; used to describe an elegant or desolate setting.
  • 漏水丁丁 - The sound of a water clock; used to describe the passage of time or quiet moments.
  • 赤阑桥 - A red-railing bridge; a common poetic image.
  • 归鸿 - A returning wild goose; a common metaphor for returning love or longing.
  • 西湖 - West Lake in Hangzhou; a famous scenic spot in Chinese poetry and culture.
  • 石阑 - A stone railing; used in poetry to describe places of contemplation or longing.
  • 钟情 - To be deeply in love; a common theme in classical poetry.
  • 玉人 - A term of endearment for a beloved, often used for a woman.
  • 翠袖笼香 - A poetic description of a woman's fragrant sleeves; symbolizes elegance and presence.
  • 玉壶温酒 - A jade vessel holding warm wine; a common image in poetry for refinement and warmth.
  • 银屏月 - Moonlight on a silver screen or mirror; a poetic image of beauty and solitude.
  • 蓄喜含嗔 - A range of emotions, from joy to anger; often used to describe a beloved's complex personality.
  • 海岳誓盟 - Vows as vast as the sea and mountains; a metaphor for enduring love.
  • 碧云春树 - Green clouds and spring trees; a poetic image of a distant and beautiful landscape.
  • 羁思 - Longing or homesickness; often used in poetry to describe the emotions of separation.
  • 伊 - A poetic term for 'her' or 'the beloved'.

[1214]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 玉漏 - Traditional Chinese water clock, symbolizing the passage of time.
  • 陽關 - Famous farewell song, often associated with parting and longing.
  • 豆蔻 - Metaphor for youth and love, derived from the 'Buddha's hand' flower (豆蔻花).
  • 桃葉渡 - Famous ferry in Nanjing, often mentioned in poetry as a place of parting and longing.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 鳳凰山 - Mountain in Nanjing, historically significant in Chinese literature and culture.
  • 浴罷畫眉 - After bathing and drawing eyebrows, symbolizing love and togetherness.
  • 夢回攜手 - Reuniting in a dream, symbolizing love and togetherness.

[1215]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 骊歌声断 - Metaphorically refers to the departure of a loved one.
  • 玉人遥 - Poetic way to describe a beautiful and distant person.
  • 孤馆寒灯 - Evokes a sense of loneliness and isolation.
  • 相思千点泪 - Expresses deep emotional pain.
  • 和雨滴红桥 - Combines rain and emotional tears.
  • 残灯暗影 - Creates an atmosphere of melancholy.
  • 别魂消 - Indicates the profound sadness of separation.
  • 泪湿鲛人玉线绡 - Metaphor for tears that are as precious as the silk of a mythical creature.
  • 云娥相送处 - Poetic reference to a beautiful and ethereal farewell.
  • 淡烟斜月 - Evokes a serene yet melancholic night scene.
  • 春衫初试 - Suggests a new beginning or a fresh start.
  • 宝凤搔头 - A decorative accessory.
  • 玉步摇 - Another ornate accessory.
  • 看灯三五夜 - Traditional festival activity.
  • 七香车子 - Luxurious vehicle.
  • 一襟拥恨 - Expresses deep sorrow and resentment.
  • 闲却鸣鸾白玉箫 - Metaphor for unused or forgotten beauty.
  • 燕子不来 - Symbol of the passage of time and the absence of loved ones.
  • 数株杨柳 - Associated with parting and nostalgia.
  • 伤春雨泪 - Expresses sorrow during the spring season.
  • 别雁离鸿 - Symbols of departure and distance.
  • 流水落花 - Metaphor for the fleeting nature of life.
  • 日斜无语 - Evokes a sense of quiet reflection.
  • 绮窗别后 - Reference to a significant moment of separation.
  • 浓睡才醒 - State of drowsiness and confusion.
  • 日午卷帘 - Moment of transition and revelation.
  • 落花飞絮 - Poetic description of springtime beauty and transience.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 红桥 - Specific bridge in a historical or literary context, often associated with romantic or melancholic scenes.
  • 云娥 - Poetic term for a beautiful and ethereal woman.
  • 七香车子 - Luxurious and ornate vehicle, often associated with high social status.
  • 绮窗 - Decorative and luxurious window, often used in classical poetry to evoke a sense of beauty and refinement.
  • 鸣鸾白玉箫 - Metaphor for a beautiful and rare musical instrument, often associated with high art and culture.

Proper Nouns

  • 张自鸿 - Proper name of a historical or literary figure.
  • 红桥 - Specific bridge with cultural significance.
  • 绮窗 - Specific type of window with cultural significance.

[1216]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 及至 - Means 'when' or 'upon reaching,' used to indicate a point in time or a stage in a process.
  • 失声号绝 - A highly emotional expression meaning 'to cry out in despair' or 'to wail uncontrollably,' indicating extreme grief.
  • 徬徨 - Means 'to wander aimlessly' or 'to be lost and uncertain,' describing a state of confusion and distress.
  • 玉珮玦 - A type of jade pendant or ornament, often symbolizing beauty or a token of remembrance.
  • 拆之 - Means 'to open it' or 'to break the seal,' referring to the act of unsealing the document.
  • 蝶恋花 - A title of a poem, often associated with love and longing.
  • 七绝句 - Refers to seven-character poems, a common form in classical Chinese poetry.
  • 漠漠梨云和梦度 - A poetic expression meaning 'the misty clouds of pear blossoms drift in dreams,' evoking a dreamlike, ethereal atmosphere.
  • 锦屏翠幙留春住 - A poetic expression meaning 'the brocade screens and green curtains hold spring captive,' suggesting the desire to retain beauty and youth.
  • 络纬泣秋风 - A poetic expression meaning 'the cicadas weep in the autumn wind,' symbolizing sorrow and the passage of time.
  • 一点残灯照药丛 - A poetic expression meaning 'a single dim light illuminates the medicinal herbs,' suggesting a sense of solitude and reflection.
  • 梦吉梦凶都不定 - A poetic expression meaning 'dreams of good and bad fortune are uncertain,' indicating the unpredictability of life.
  • 朝朝望断北来鸿 - A poetic expression meaning 'every day, I gaze northward, hoping for a message from afar,' symbolizing longing and anticipation.
  • 井落金瓶信不通 - A poetic expression meaning 'the well drops gold bottles, but messages cannot be conveyed,' suggesting a sense of isolation and communication barriers.
  • 云山渺渺暗丹枫 - A poetic expression meaning 'the distant mountains and red maples are shrouded in mist,' evoking a sense of distance and melancholy.
  • 轻罗暗湿鸳鸯冷 - A poetic expression meaning 'the light silk is damp, and the mandarin ducks feel cold,' suggesting a sense of loneliness and desolation.
  • 闲听长宵嘹唳鸿 - A poetic expression meaning 'listening idly to the long night, the cries of the geese echo,' symbolizing solitude and the passage of time.
  • 寂寂香闺枕簟空 - A poetic expression meaning 'the quiet fragrant chamber is empty, the bed and mat are unused,' suggesting a sense of loneliness and abandonment.
  • 满阶秋雨落梧桐 - A poetic expression meaning 'the courtyard is covered with autumn rain, the parasol trees are wet,' evoking a sense of melancholy and the passage of time.
  • 内家不遣园陵去 - A poetic expression meaning 'the inner chambers do not send messages to the imperial tombs,' suggesting a sense of isolation and the inability to communicate.
  • 音信何缘寄塞鸿 - A poetic expression meaning 'how can messages be entrusted to the wild geese crossing the frontier,' symbolizing the difficulty of communication and longing.
  • 玉箸双垂满颊红 - A poetic expression meaning 'tears flow down the cheeks, turning them red,' suggesting intense emotion and sorrow.
  • 关山何处寄书筒 - A poetic expression meaning 'where can letters be sent across the mountains and passes,' symbolizing the difficulty of communication and longing.
  • 绿窗寂寞无人到 - A poetic expression meaning 'the green window is lonely, no one comes,' suggesting a sense of isolation and abandonment.
  • 海阔天高怨落鸿 - A poetic expression meaning 'the sea is vast and the sky is high, the geese that have fallen are resented,' symbolizing the vastness of the world and the pain of separation.
  • 衾寒翡翠怯秋风 - A poetic expression meaning 'the quilt is cold, the emerald green birds are afraid of the autumn wind,' suggesting a sense of vulnerability and the passage of time.
  • 郎在天南妾在东 - A poetic expression meaning 'the husband is in the south, the wife is in the east,' symbolizing separation and longing.
  • 相见千回都是梦 - A poetic expression meaning 'meeting thousands of times is still a dream,' suggesting the unreality and fleeting nature of their relationship.
  • 楼头长日妒双鸿 - A poetic expression meaning 'the long days on the tower envy the pair of geese,' symbolizing the envy of those who are separated and the longing for reunion.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 红桥 - Refers to a famous bridge in Beijing, known for its romantic associations and historical significance.
  • 张已卒 - Refers to the death of a person named Zhang, likely a significant figure in the story.
  • 床头玉珮玦 - A jade pendant or ornament, often given as a token of remembrance or love.
  • 蝶恋花 - A title of a poem, often associated with themes of love and longing.
  • 七绝句 - Refers to seven-character poems, a common form in classical Chinese poetry.
  • 金瓶 - A type of container, often used in literature to symbolize messages or gifts.
  • 鸳鸯 - A symbol of love and fidelity, often used in poetry to represent a married couple.
  • 锦屏翠幙 - Refers to luxurious screens and curtains, often used to create a sense of privacy and luxury.
  • 内家 - Refers to the inner chambers or private quarters, often associated with the imperial court.
  • 园陵 - Refers to imperial tombs or mausoleums, often used to symbolize the afterlife or the impermanence of life.
  • 塞鸿 - Refers to wild geese that migrate across the frontier, often used as a symbol of messages or long-distance communication.

Proper Nouns

  • 红桥 - The Red Bridge in Beijing.
  • 张 - A person named Zhang, likely a significant figure in the story.
  • 蝶恋花 - The title of a poem.
  • 七绝句 - Seven-character poems.
  • 金瓶 - A type of container.
  • 鸳鸯 - A symbol of love and fidelity.
  • 锦屏翠幙 - Luxurious screens and curtains.
  • 内家 - Inner chambers or private quarters.
  • 园陵 - Imperial tombs or mausoleums.
  • 塞鸿 - Wild geese that migrate across the frontier.

[1217]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 一南一北似飘蓬 - Metaphor comparing the separation of two people to the drifting of tumbleweeds, symbolizing their aimless and sorrowful state.
  • 妾意君心恨不同 - Poetic expression indicating that the speaker and her lover have different feelings, leading to mutual sorrow.
  • 他日归来也无益 - Phrase suggesting that returning in the future would be futile or meaningless.
  • 夜台应少系书鸿 - Metaphor where '夜台' (the underworld) and '系书鸿' (a messenger bird carrying a letter) imply that even in the afterlife, there will be no communication or hope of reunion.
  • 柔肠百结泪悬河 - Vivid description of the speaker's intense sorrow, with '柔肠百结' (a tangle of soft intestines) and '泪悬河' (tears hanging like a river) emphasizing her emotional pain.
  • 瘗玉埋香 - Metaphor for the burial of a beautiful and fragrant object, symbolizing the loss of something precious.
  • 明月也知留佩玦 - Poetic expression where the moon is personified as knowing to keep the speaker's jade ring, symbolizing the moon's awareness of her sorrow.
  • 晓来长想画青蛾 - Phrase where '画青蛾' (painted eyebrows) refers to the deceased's beauty, and the speaker longs to remember her.

  • 望平原兮太息 - Looking upon the plain, I sighed deeply; image of longing or loss.
  • 涕泗横兮沾臆 - Tears and snot flowed, soaking my chest; vivid expression of deep sorrow or grief.

  • 望子来兮秋树零 - I wait for you to return, as autumn trees wither; contrast with previous line, expressing longing and loss.
  • 树有枝兮枝有英 - Trees have branches, and branches have blossoms; poetic description of nature, possibly symbolic of relationship.
  • 我胡为兮茕茕 - Why am I alone, so lonely?; rhetorical question expressing loneliness or despair.
  • 子在此兮山城 - You are here, in the mountain city; indicates the location of the absent person.
  • 织女兮牛郎 - Reference to the Chinese mythological figures *Zhinü* (Weaving Girl) and *Niulang* (Cowherd).

  • 欲经渡兮河无梁 - I wish to cross the river, but there is no bridge; metaphor for unbridgeable separation or impossible journey.
  • 霜露侵袭兮病偃在床 - Frost and dew invaded, and I lay ill in bed; describes physical illness and suffering.
  • 嗟嗟夫子兮谁与缝裳 - Alas, my husband, who will sew my clothes?; lament for the loss of a spouse.
  • 宗道卒不听 - Zongdao finally did not listen; indicates resistance or refusal to heed a plea.
  • 妾委蜕矣，子盍送我 - I have shed my body, you should send me; *委蜕* refers to the shedding of the physical body or the soul leaving the body.
  • 讣至 - The news of death arrived; *讣* is a formal notice of death.
  • 宗道未几悲死 - Zongdao did not long survive, dying in sorrow; died shortly after the event, likely from grief.
  • 冯爱生 - A proper name, likely of a courtesan or woman in the story; *冯* is the surname, *爱生* may mean 'loving life' or 'beloved'.
  • 龙子犹《爱生传》云 - According to Long Ziyou’s *Biography of Aisheng*; *龙子犹* is the author, possibly a pen name or historical figure.
  • 爱生非吴产，亦不审谁姓 - Aisheng was not from Wu, and her family name is unknown; indicates unclear origins and lineage.
  • 年十四，或鬻于金阊之冯妪家 - At fourteen, she was sold to the household of Madam Feng in Jincha; *鬻* means 'to sell', *金阊* refers to Jincha, an area in Suzhou.
  • 冯累世为青楼冠 - Madam Feng's family had for generations been the top of the courtesan houses; *青楼* refers to brothels or houses of courtesans.
  • 吴语一时呼某姬曰某生 - In Wu dialect, people at the time called a courtesan by the name *Sheng*; reflects a linguistic or cultural convention in the region.
  • 妪产四女，皆名姬，而季名喜者尤著 - Madam Feng had four daughters, all of whom were courtesans, and the youngest, named Xi, was especially famous.
  • 四姬以次适人，妪意亦怠 - The four daughters married in succession, and Madam Feng's interest waned; indicates loss of former prominence.
  • 而其妇八娘子主家，新寡，得爱生女之 - But her wife, Madam Baxiang, who now was a widow, adopted Aisheng as her daughter; *主家* refers to the head of the household, *新寡* means 'newly widowed'.

  • 青楼 - Refers to brothels or courtesan houses, often associated with high culture and literary patronage in classical China.
  • 金阊 - A district in Suzhou, historically known for its cultural and commercial significance, especially in the context of courtesans and literature.
  • 冯妪家 - The household of Madam Feng, a powerful and influential courtesan family, likely from the Jiangnan region.
  • 龙子犹 - A likely pseudonym or historical figure, possibly a writer or compiler of this biography.
  • 八娘子 - A title for a woman in a household, possibly the wife or mistress of the household.
  • 适人 - To be married off, a common practice for courtesans and women in classical China.
  • 新寡 - A newly widowed woman, often with a position of authority in the household.
  • 主家 - The head of the household, typically a woman in the case of courtesan families.

Proper Nouns

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 阿母桃花方似锦，王孙草色正如烟 - Poetic description using '阿母' (A mother) and '王孙' (prince or noble) metaphorically. '桃花方似锦' (peach blossoms are like brocade) and '草色正如烟' (grass color is like smoke) vividly describe natural beauty.
  • 不须更向沧溟望，惆怅欢娱恰一年 - Uses '沧溟' (vast ocean) metaphorically to suggest no need to seek further. '惆怅欢娱恰一年' (sorrow and joy have been exactly one year) indicates a period of mixed emotions.
  • 东美何故频见梦中乎 - Rhetorical question asking why '东美' (Dongmei) frequently appears in dreams.
  • 素服拜奠，抚柩哀号，一恸而绝 - '素服拜奠' (paying respects in plain clothes), '抚柩哀号' (embracing the coffin and wailing), and '一恸而绝' (dying from a single great grief) describe actions and emotions associated with mourning.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 薛到外国，未行册礼，旌节晓夕有声，旋染疾 - '薛' (Xue) is a person's name, '外国' (foreign country) refers to a place outside the current domain. '册礼' (imperial investiture ceremony) is a formal ritual for bestowing titles or honors. '旌节' (banner and staff) are symbols of authority and rank.
  • 苗摄大使行礼 - '苗' (Miao) is another person's name, '摄大使' (acting as the ambassador) indicates that he temporarily took on the role of an official.
  • 薛旅榇回及青州，东美乃请告至驿，素服拜奠，抚柩哀号，一恸而绝 - '青州' (Qingzhou) is a place name, '请告至驿' (requesting leave to go to the post station) shows the act of traveling to pay respects.
  • 薄少君，娄东秀士沈承妻也 - '薄少君' (Bo Shaojun) is a person's name, '娄东秀士沈承' (Loudong scholar Shen Cheng) refers to a man named Shen Cheng, who was a scholar from Loudong.
  • 薄为诗百首悼之 - '薄' (Bo) is the name of the woman, '诗百首' (hundred poems) indicates the extent of her mourning poetry.
  • 及期，少君亦逝 - '及期' (by the time) indicates that the event happened at a specific point, '少君亦逝' (Shaojun also passed away) shows that she died around the same time.

  • 赓句联愁 - Means 'to continue the verses and share the sorrow,' indicating shared emotional burdens.

  • 九泉衔怨 - Means 'to hold grudges in the underworld,' indicating deep-seated resentment.

  • 终身五内 - Means 'to live with deep sorrow,' indicating lasting emotional pain.

  • 犹属幻想之依稀也 - Means 'still belongs to the faint traces of fantasy,' indicating the ephemeral nature of illusions.
  • 幸而花植幽房，剑归烈士 - Means 'fortunately, the flower is planted in a secluded room, and the sword returns to the brave,' suggesting a positive outcome in a secluded or heroic context.
  • 两情相喻 - Means 'mutual understanding,' indicating deep emotional connection.
  • 永好勿谖 - Means 'forever good and never forget,' indicating a lasting and unbroken bond.

Similarly, '方将居之' indicates a future intention that must be understood in the context of the conversation, and '唯唯' shows agreement and must be read in the context of the dialogue.
  • Other phrases like '愿偿平生之志耳' express a personal ambition and must be understood in the context of the protagonist's goals, while '男女之际，大欲存焉' describes a natural human desire and must be read in the context of the relationship between the protagonists. '情苟相得，虽父母不能制也' emphasizes the strength of mutual feelings and must be read in the context of the relationship dynamics. '愿以身为厮养' expresses a willingness to serve and must be understood in the context of the protagonist's humility. '召家僮，请以双缣，备一宵之馔' describes the preparation of a meal and must be read in the context of hospitality. '撤馔' marks the end of a meal and must be understood in the context of the narrative sequence. '叙邂逅相慕之意' describes the exchange of feelings and must be read in the context of the relationship development. '宿何害焉' reassures the other party and must be understood in the context of the conversation. '日会倡优辈狎戏' describes social activities and must be read in the context of the protagonist's lifestyle. '囊中渐铄' indicates financial depletion and must be understood in the context of the protagonist's situation. '资斧荡然' describes financial ruin and must be read in the context of the protagonist's circumstances. '情弥笃' describes a deepening of emotional bonds and must be understood in the context of the relationship. Idiomatic Expressions

  • 新安人数问媪期，辄曰：“未未。” - 新安人 refers to a person from the region of Xin'an (modern-day Anhui Province). 辄曰：“未未。” is a colloquial expression meaning 'he always says 'not yet, not yet.' The repetition of '未' emphasizes uncertainty or delay.
  • 及至秋月，过谓媪曰：“初谋柳下，条叶未黄，约及垂阴，子已成实。过此渐秃，行将白雪侵枝矣！ - 及至秋月 means 'by the time autumn arrived.' 初谋柳下 refers to an initial plan or arrangement under a willow tree. 条叶未黄 means 'the branches and leaves were not yet yellow,' symbolizing the early stage of something. 约及垂阴 means 'until the shade is fully cast,' implying a period of growth or development. 子已成实 means 'the fruit has already ripened,' symbolizing the completion of a plan. 过此渐秃，行将白雪侵枝矣 means 'after this, it will gradually become bare, and soon snow will cover the branches,' symbolizing decline or the end of a cycle.
  • 媪曰：“今夕随老身入，须着精神，成败系此。不然，虚废半年也。” - 今夕随老身入 means 'this evening, follow me inside.' 须着精神 means 'you must be alert and focused.' 成败系此 means 'the success or failure depends on this,' emphasizing the importance of the moment. 不然，虚废半年也 means 'otherwise, half a year will be wasted in vain,' highlighting the urgency of the situation.
  • 因授之计。 - 因授之计 means 'then she gave him a plan,' indicating that the woman provided a strategy or method.
  • 媪每夜黑至妇家，是夕，阴与新安人同入，而伏之寝门之外。 - 每夜黑至妇家 means 'every night she would go to the woman's house in the dark.' 阴与新安人同入 means 'secretly with the Xin'an man, she entered together.' 而伏之寝门之外 means 'and she hid outside the bedroom door,' indicating a strategic position.
  • 媪与妇酌于房，两声甚戚，笑剧加殷。 - 酌于房 means 'they drank in the room,' indicating a social or intimate setting. 两声甚戚 means 'their voices were very sad,' suggesting a melancholic or serious tone. 笑剧加殷 means 'they laughed and joked more earnestly,' indicating a shift in mood.
  • 媪强侍儿酒，侍儿不胜，醉卧他所。 - 强侍儿酒 means 'she forced the maid to drink,' indicating coercion or insistence. 不胜，醉卧他所 means 'the maid couldn't handle it and fell asleep elsewhere,' suggesting the maid's inability to cope.
  • 独两人闭门深饮，各已微酣。 - 独两人闭门深饮 means 'the two of them closed the door and drank deeply,' indicating privacy and indulgence. 各已微酣 means 'they were both slightly tipsy,' suggesting a state of intoxication.
  • 适有飞蛾来火上，媪佯以扇扑之，灯灭，伪启门点灯，复佯笑曰：“忘携烛去。” - 适有飞蛾来火上 means 'just then, a moth flew to the flame,' indicating a sudden event. 媪佯以扇扑之 means 'the woman pretended to swat it with a fan,' suggesting a feigned action. 灯灭，伪启门点灯 means 'the lamp went out, and she pretended to open the door to relight it,' indicating a ruse. 复佯笑曰：“忘携烛去。” means 'she pretended to laugh and said, 'I forgot to bring a candle,' suggesting a cover-up.
  • 折旋之际，则已暗导其人于卧榻矣。 - 折旋之际 means 'at the turn,' indicating a moment of opportunity. 则已暗导其人于卧榻矣 means 'she had already secretly led the man to the bed,' suggesting a hidden maneuver.
  • 顷之，辞以夜深火尽，复闭门。 - 顷之 means 'after a while,' indicating a short period of time. 辞以夜深火尽 means 'she excused herself by saying it was too late and the fire had gone out,' suggesting a reason for leaving.
  • 妇畏暗，数数呼媪。 - 妇畏暗 means 'the woman was afraid of the dark,' indicating a fear or discomfort. 数数呼媪 means 'she repeatedly called for the woman,' suggesting a need for reassurance.
  • 媪曰：“老身当同帷作伴耳。” - 老身当同帷作伴耳 means 'I will stay with you in the same tent,' indicating a promise of companionship.
  • 乃挟其人登妇床，妇犹以为媪也，启被抚其身，曰：“姥体滑如是！” - 乃挟其人登妇床 means 'she then took the man and led him to the woman's bed,' indicating a physical action. 妇犹以为媪也 means 'the woman still thought it was the woman,' suggesting a mistaken identity. 启被抚其身，曰：“姥体滑如是！” means 'she pulled back the covers and touched his body, saying, 'Your body is so smooth!'' indicating a physical interaction.
  • 其人不言，腾身而上，妇已神狂，听其轻薄而已。 - 其人不言，腾身而上 means 'the man said nothing and climbed up,' indicating a passive or submissive action. 妇已神狂，听其轻薄而已 means 'the woman was already in a trance and let him do as he pleased,' suggesting a state of euphoria or surrender.

  • 妇业堕术中，遂不能舍，相爱逾于夫妇。 - 妇业堕术中 means 'the woman had already fallen into the trap,' indicating a loss of control or agency. 遂不能舍 means 'she could no longer let go,' suggesting a state of addiction or obsession. 相爱逾于夫妇 means 'they loved each other more than a husband and wife,' indicating a deep emotional connection.

  • 何不遣媒礼聘，成百年之眷乎？ - 'Why not send a matchmaker to formally propose, and establish a lifelong union?' This is a formal suggestion for marriage.
  • 鸾点头称是 - 'Luan nodded in agreement.' This is a narrative expression of approval.
  • 生乃伪托父命，求婚于王 - 'The scholar then falsely claimed to have received his father’s command and proposed to Lord Wang.' This is a narrative of deception.
  • 王亦雅重生 - 'Lord Wang also greatly respected the scholar.' This is a phrase indicating admiration or high regard.
  • 但爱女不欲远嫁他乡，迟疑未许 - 'But he loved his daughter and did not want her to marry far away, so he hesitated and did not agree.' This is a narrative of internal conflict.
  • 生遂设计，托以衙斋窄狭，假卫署后园肄业 - 'The scholar then devised a plan, claiming that his official residence was too small, and pretended to study in the garden behind the military office.' This is a narrative of a fabricated excuse.
  • 且以周夫人同姓，请拜为姑 - 'And because the Zhou family shared the same surname, he requested to be considered her uncle-in-law.' This is a formal statement about familial relations.
  • 王，武人，喜于奉承，许之，且愿任饔飧 - 'Lord Wang, a military man, liked flattery and agreed, and was even willing to provide food and lodging.' This is a narrative of persuasion and acceptance.
  • 周遂寓居园亭，因得以兄妹之礼见鸾，情愈亲密 - 'Zhou then lived in the garden, and was thus able to meet Luan in the manner of siblings, and their feelings became even closer.' This is a narrative of increased intimacy.
  • 而曹姨居间，以盟主自任，先立婚誓，始订幽期 - 'While Aunt Cao acted as an intermediary, taking it upon herself to be the matchmaker, first establishing a marriage vow, and then setting a secret appointment.' This is a narrative of the matchmaker’s role.
  • 从此绸缪无间，恩逾夫妇 - 'From then on, they were inseparable and their love surpassed that of husband and wife.' This is a poetic expression of deep affection.

  • 吾女亦不為無過 - My daughter is also not without fault; 無過 means 'without fault' or 'blameless.'
  • 若能遂為婚，吾亦不復言 - If you can marry her, I will not say another word; 遂為婚 means to proceed with the marriage.
  • 叩頭謝罪，願從命 - He fell to his knees and begged for forgiveness, willing to obey; 叩頭 is to bow with the head touching the ground; 歲從命 is to be willing to comply with the command.
  • 既成婚，夫婦相得甚歡 - After the marriage, the couple were very happy with each other; 相得 means to get along well or be compatible.

  • 長安 - The capital of the Han dynasty and many other dynasties, located in present-day Xi’an.
  • 中牟 - A county in Henan province, historically known for its role in the Warring States and Han periods.
  • 主簿 - A government official, typically the assistant to the county magistrate or higher official.
  • 焦大郎 - A name, possibly a commoner or minor official. The name 大郎 is a common honorific or nickname for a man, often used in classical and vernacular texts.
  • 室女 - A daughter, specifically a maiden or unmarried woman.
  • 士君子 - A classical term for a virtuous, educated, and morally upright man, often used to contrast with those of lower moral standing.
  • 叩頭 - A traditional form of showing respect or apology, involving bowing deeply with the head touching the ground.

[1295]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 如是幾二十年 - Classical expression meaning 'In this way, for nearly twenty years,' indicating a period of time passed in a particular manner or condition.
  • 累官鸿胪少卿 - Term used to describe a gradual promotion through successive official posts. '累官' means 'to be promoted step by step,' and '鸿胪少卿' is a specific official title in the imperial bureaucracy.
  • 出知齐州 - Classical expression meaning 'was dispatched to serve as the governor of Qi Prefecture.' '出知' is a common formula for being assigned to a local administrative post.
  • 视印三日 - Literally 'to look at the seal for three days,' but in context, it likely means 'to take up the post and examine the seal (i.e., the official duties) for three days.' This is a metaphorical way of saying he was just beginning his new role.
  • 偶携家人子散步后堂 - Phrase meaning 'happened to take his family members for a walk in the rear hall.' The use of '偶' suggests a chance or incidental occurrence.
  • 逢生辄趋避 - 'Whenever they met him, they would turn away.' '辄' indicates a habitual reaction.
  • 生迫视之 - 'The man looked at them closely.' '迫视' means to stare intently or look closely.
  • 一妇人着冠帔 - 'A woman wearing a cap and robe.' This is a description of formal or semi-formal attire, likely indicating a woman of status or dignity.
  • 褰帷出 - 'Pulled aside the curtain and came out.' '褰帷' is a classical expression for opening a curtain or veil.
  • 生惶惧失措 - 'The man was filled with fear and panic.' '惶惧' is a compound term for fear and alarm; '失措' means to lose composure.
  • 焦泫然泣曰 - 'Jiao wept tearfully and said.' '泫然' is a poetic term for weeping with emotion.
  • 一别二十年 - 'We have been separated for twenty years.' A common expression used in classical Chinese to express the length of time since separation.
  • 向来婉娈之情，略不相念 - 'You have not at all remembered the tender feelings of the past.' '婉娈' is a classical term for tender or affectionate feelings.
  • 汝真忍人也 - 'You are truly cruel.' '忍人' is a term used to describe someone who is heartless or cruel.
  • 不暇叩其所从来 - 'Had no time to ask where she had come from.' '不暇' means 'had no time' or 'was too preoccupied.'
  • 具以实告 - 'Told the truth in full.' A formal expression for revealing all the facts.
  • 吾知之久矣 - 'I have known this for a long time.' A common expression of long-standing awareness.
  • 吾父已死，兄弟不肖，乡里无所依 - 'My father is already dead, my brothers are unworthy, and I have no place to rely on in the village.' This is a lamentation expressing a state of abandonment and loss.
  • 千里相投 - 'Traveled a thousand miles to seek refuge.' A metaphorical expression for a long journey to seek help or shelter.
  • 为阍者所拒 - 'Was rejected by the gatekeeper.' '阍者' refers to the gatekeeper or porter.
  • 恳祈再三，仅得托足 - 'After repeated pleas, I was only allowed to find a place to rest.' '托足' is an idiom meaning to find a place to rest or settle.
  • 今一身孤单，茫无栖泊 - 'Now I am alone and have no place to rest.' '茫无栖泊' is a poetic expression for being without a place to belong.
  • 汝既有嘉偶，吾得备侧室 - 'Since you already have a good wife, I can be your concubine.' '侧室' refers to a concubine or secondary wife.
  • 竟此余生，以奉事君子及尊夫人足矣 - 'I would be content to spend the rest of my life serving you and your esteemed wife.' '竟此余生' means 'to complete the rest of my life.'
  • 前事不复较也 - 'I will not dwell on the past.' '不复较' means 'no longer to compare' or 'not to dwell on.'
  • 语毕长恸 - 'After finishing her words, she wept bitterly.' '长恸' is a term for a long, sorrowful cry or wail.
  • 生软语慰藉之 - 'The man gently comforted her.' '软语' is a term for gentle or soothing words.
  • 且畏彰闻于外 - 'And also feared that it would be known outside.' '彰闻' means to become known or public.
  • 乃以语朱氏 - 'So he told Zhu.' This is a narrative formula used to indicate that he shared the information with Zhu.
  • 朱素贤淑，欣然迎归，待之如妹 - 'Zhu was always virtuous and kind, and she joyfully welcomed her back, treating her like a sister.' This is a formal description of Zhu’s character and actions.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 鸿胪少卿 - A high-ranking official in the imperial court, responsible for foreign affairs and ceremonies. The *Honglu* (鸿胪) was an official responsible for the reception of foreign envoys and the organization of imperial rituals.
  • 齐州 - A historical prefecture in Shandong, known today as Jinan. It was an important administrative and cultural center in ancient China.
  • 视印三日 - A metaphor for beginning a new post, as the seal was a symbol of authority. In ancient China, the official seal was a key symbol of a person's authority and responsibility.
  • 家人子 - Refers to the man’s family members, likely including his wife and children.
  • 青衣 - A term for a servant or attendant, typically dressed in blue robes. In classical Chinese literature, this term often appears in narrative contexts to describe servants or attendants.
  • 别院右舍 - Refers to a separate courtyard or wing of a house, indicating a compound or large estate.
  • 冠帔 - A formal attire for women, consisting of a cap (*guan*) and a robe (*pei*). This was the attire of a married woman or a woman of status.
  • 褰帷 - To lift or pull aside a curtain or veil. This is a poetic way of describing someone emerging from behind a curtain, often used in narrative contexts.
  • 焦氏 - The surname of the woman, likely a reference to a character from a classical story or novel, though not a historical figure.
  • 生 - A classical term used to refer to the male protagonist or narrator in a narrative, often used in classical fiction.
  • 侧室 - A concubine or secondary wife. In classical Chinese society, a man could have multiple wives, with the primary wife being the *zhu* (正室) and the secondary wife being the *ce shi* (侧室).
  • 嘉偶 - A term for a good or virtuous wife. It is often used in classical literature to describe a harmonious or ideal marriage.
  • 朱氏 - The surname of the woman, likely the main wife or wife of the protagonist.
  • 贤淑 - A term used to describe a virtuous and kind woman, often used in classical literature to describe a wife or mother.
  • 迎归 - To welcome back. This term is used to describe the act of bringing someone back into the household, especially after a long separation or hardship.
  • 待之如妹 - To treat her like a sister. This indicates that the wife (朱氏) is not only accepting of the concubine but also treating her with familial warmth and affection.

  • 负言 - Means 'to go back on one's word' or 'to break a promise.'
  • 因以刀割其阳 - Means 'then cut off his male organ with a knife.' '因' (yīn) indicates causality, and '阳' (yáng) refers to the male organ.
  • 虽不致死 - Means 'although it did not result in death.' '虽' (suī) is a concessive particle, and '致死' (zhì sǐ) means 'to cause death.'
  • 人性永废 - Means 'his human nature was permanently ruined.' '人性' (rén xìng) refers to human nature or character, and '永废' (yǒng fèi) means 'permanently ruined.'
  • 张夫人 - Refers to the wife of Zhang Zineng. '夫人' (fū rén) is a respectful term for a wife.

  • 吳少帝 - Refers to Emperor Sun Xiu (孫休) of the Eastern Wu dynasty, who reigned from 258 to 264 AD.
  • 五鳳元年 - The first year of the Wufeng era, which was the fifth era name of Emperor Sun Xiu. This corresponds to 256 AD.
  • 會稽 - A historical region and prefecture in eastern China, roughly corresponding to modern-day Zhejiang Province.
  • 百姓 - Refers to common people or ordinary citizens.
  • 室女 - A young unmarried woman.
  • 貌美 - Means 'beautiful appearance.'
  • 邻里少年求娶者颇众 - Means 'many young men from the neighborhood sought to marry her.'
  • 父母惜而不嫁 - Means 'her parents were reluctant to let her marry.'
  • 姿貌玉洁 - Describes someone with a beautiful and pure appearance.
  • 愿婚此女 - Means 'wishes to marry this daughter.'
  • 纳聘财 - Refers to the payment of a dowry or betrothal gifts.
  • 成婚媾 - Means 'to consummate the marriage.'
  • 有孕 - Means 'to become pregnant.'
  • 十二月 - Refers to the twelfth month of the lunar calendar.
  • 如绢囊 - Means 'like a silk bag.'
  • 大如升 - Means 'as large as a winnowing basket.'
  • 悉是鱼子 - Means 'all were fish eggs.'
  • 吾不幸，故产此异物 - Means 'unfortunately, I have given birth to this strange thing.'
  • 鳞甲之状 - Refers to the appearance of scales and armor.
  • 巨石镇之 - Means 'to crush it with a large stone.'
  • 异常诟骂 - Means 'to curse abnormally.'
  • 偃踣 - Means 'to fall down.'
  • 声震于外 - Means 'the sound was heard outside.'
  • 床下有白鱼，长六七尺 - Means 'there was a white fish under the bed, six or seven feet long.'
  • 未死，在地拨剌 - Means 'still alive, wriggling on the ground.'
  • 砍断之，投江中 - Means 'cut it in half and threw it into the river.'
  • 别嫁 - Means 'to marry again.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 吴少帝五凤元年四月 - Refers to the first month of the Wufeng era during the reign of Emperor Sun Xiu of the Eastern Wu dynasty.
  • 江郎 - A name given to the supernatural being who appeared as a young man.
  • 白鱼怪 - Refers to a supernatural creature in the form of a fish, which is a common motif in Chinese folklore and mythology.
  • 鱼子 - Fish eggs, which are mentioned as the unusual offspring of the '江郎' and the girl.
  • 江中 - Refers to a river or lake, likely the Qiantang River, which flows through Zhejiang Province.
  • 别嫁 - The practice of remarrying after the death of a spouse, which was common in ancient China.

Proper Nouns

  • 吴少帝 - Emperor Sun Xiu of the Eastern Wu dynasty.
  • 五凤元年 - The first year of the Wufeng era (256 AD).
  • 会稽 - A historical region and prefecture in eastern China.
  • 江郎 - A name given to the supernatural being.
  • 王素 - The father of the girl.
  • 白鱼怪 - A supernatural creature in the form of a fish.

[1384]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 不乐嫁俗人 - Means 'not willing to marry an ordinary person,' indicating that the spirit or demon does not want to marry a human.
  • 术咒疗 - Means 'to use spells and incantations to treat.'
  • 分死 - Means 'to know it will die,' reflecting a fatalistic view.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 永初中 - The early years of the Yonghe era (107-113 AD) during the Eastern Han dynasty.
  • 武昌太守 - The governor of Wuchang, a significant administrative position.
  • 嫁女 - Traditional Chinese wedding customs, where the bride would enter the groom's carriage.
  • 邪魅 - Common belief in evil spirits and demons in ancient China.
  • 击鼓 - Ritual practice to ward off evil spirits.
  • 欺惑百姓 - Reference to the role of officials in maintaining public order and preventing deception.
  • 刻期 - Setting deadlines for official matters.
  • 朱书龟背 - Writing on the turtle's back, often associated with divination and magic.
  • 辞诀 - Farewell rituals, emphasizing the importance of goodbyes in Chinese culture.
  • 失其姻好 - Loss of a marriage alliance, highlighting the significance of marriage in ancient Chinese society.
  • 魅者归于一物 - Concept of spirits returning to a single form, reflecting beliefs in reincarnation or transformation.

Proper Nouns

  • 张春 - Zhang Chun, the governor of Wuchang.
  • 武昌 - Wuchang, a city in present-day Hubei province.
  • 永初中 - The early years of the Yonghe era (107-113 AD).
  • 青蛇 - A green snake.
  • 大龟 - A large turtle.
  • 白鼍 - A white crocodile.

[1385]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 舒信道中丞 - A title and name. '舒信道' is the name of a person, and '中丞' is an official title, likely referring to a mid-level official or vice-governor.
  • 宅在明州 - '宅' means residence or house, '明州' is a historical name for a region in present-day Zhejiang Province, China.
  • 負城濒湖 - '負城' means 'backing against the city' or 'situated near the city wall,' and '濒湖' means 'beside a lake.'
  • 繞屋皆古木茂竹 - '繞屋' means 'surrounding the house,' '古木' is 'ancient trees,' and '茂竹' is 'lush bamboo.'
  • 蕭森如山麓間 - '蕭森' conveys a sense of cool, desolate, and quiet atmosphere, while '山麓間' means 'between the foothills.'
  • 便座曰懒堂 - '便座' refers to a private or leisurely seat or room, and '懒堂' is the name of the room.
  • 背有大池 - '背' means 'behind,' and '大池' is 'large pond' or 'big pool.'
  • 子弟群處講習 - '子弟' refers to the children or younger members of the family, '群處' is 'gather together,' and '講習' means 'to study and discuss.'
  • 外客不得至 - '外客' refers to outsiders or visitors, and '不得至' means 'are not allowed to enter.'
  • 方盛秋 - '方' means 'just when' or 'at the time when,' and '盛秋' means 'late autumn' or 'the height of autumn.'
  • 新月出 - '新月' is 'new moon,' and '出' is 'appears' or 'rises.'
  • 忽見女子揭帘入 - '忽見' means 'suddenly saw,' '女子' is 'woman' or 'girl,' '揭帘' is 'pulled up the curtain,' and '入' is 'entered.'
  • 素衣淡妆 - '素衣' is 'plain or white clothing,' and '淡妆' is 'light makeup.'
  • 微有悲涕容 - '微有' is 'slightly has,' '悲涕' is 'tears of sorrow,' and '容' is 'expression' or 'appearance.'
  • 緩步而前曰 - '緩步' is 'slowly walking,' '而前' is 'forward,' and '曰' is 'said.'
  • 竊慕君子少年高志 - '竊慕' is 'secretly admire,' '君子' is 'gentleman' or 'virtuous person,' '少年' is 'young man,' and '高志' is 'noble aspirations.'
  • 欲冥行相奔 - '欲' is 'want to,' '冥行' is 'to walk in darkness' or 'to proceed without light,' and '相奔' is 'to run toward each other' or 'to seek each other out.'
  • 願容駐片時 - '願' is 'wish,' '容' is 'allow,' '駐' is 'stay,' and '片時' is 'a short time.'
  • 使奉款曲 - '使' is 'so that,' '奉' is 'serve' or 'offer,' and '款曲' is 'affection' or 'closeness.'
  • 舒迷蒙恍恍 - '舒' is the name, '迷蒙' is 'dazed' or 'confused,' and '恍恍' is 'dazzled' or 'in a trance.'
  • 不疑為異物 - '不疑' is 'did not suspect,' and '異物' is 'strange thing' or 'otherworldly being.'
  • 即與語 - '即' is 'then,' and '與語' is 'spoke with her.'
  • 扣其姓氏所居 - '扣' is 'asked,' '姓氏' is 'surname and name,' and '所居' is 'where she lives.'
  • 妾本邱氏 - '妾' is 'I' or 'myself,' used by women in formal speech, '本' is 'originally,' and '邱氏' is the surname 'Qiu.'
  • 父作商賈 - '父' is 'father,' '作' is 'was,' and '商賈' is 'merchant' or 'trader.'
  • 死于湖南 - '死' is 'died,' and '湖南' is 'Hunan Province.'
  • 但與繼母居茆茨小屋 - '但' is 'but' or 'only,' '與' is 'with,' '繼母' is 'stepmother,' '居' is 'live in,' '茆茨' is 'mud and thatch,' and '小屋' is 'small house.'
  • 相去只一二里 - '相去' is 'distance between,' '只' is 'only,' and '一二里' is 'one or two li' (a Chinese unit of distance).
  • 母残忍猛暴 - '母' is 'mother,' '殘忍' is 'cruel,' and '猛暴' is 'violent' or 'brutal.'
  • 不能見存 - '不能見存' could mean 'unable to tolerate my presence' or 'unable to allow me to live.'
  • 又不使媒妁議姻 - '又' is 'also,' '不使' is 'did not allow,' '媒妁' is 'matchmakers,' and '議姻' is 'to arrange marriage.'
  • 無故捶擊 - '無故' is 'without cause' or 'for no reason,' and '捶擊' is 'beat or strike.'
  • 以刀相吓 - '以刀' is 'with a knife,' and '相吓' is 'to threaten.'
  • 急走逃命 - '急走' is 'fled quickly,' and '逃命' is 'to escape for one's life.'
  • 勢難復歸 - '勢' is 'circumstances' or 'situation,' and '難復歸' is 'difficult to return.'
  • 倘得蓄為婢子 - '倘' is 'if,' '得' is 'can,' '蓄' is 'keep' or 'retain,' and '婢子' is 'maid' or 'female servant.'
  • 固所大願 - '固' is 'indeed,' and '所大願' is 'my greatest wish.'
  • 俄一小青衣携酒肴来 - '俄' is 'soon,' '一小青衣' is 'a young girl in green clothing,' and '携酒肴来' is 'brought wine and food.'
  • 即促膝共飲 - '即' is 'then,' '促膝' is 'sit close together.'

Proper Nouns

  • 明州 - Historical name for a region in present-day Zhejiang Province, China.
  • 湖南 - Hunan Province.
  • 邱氏 - Surname 'Qiu.'

[1386]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 绿净湖光 - Evokes a tranquil, picturesque scene; 'Green and clear lake light' or 'the serene beauty of the green lake.'
  • 浅寒先到芙蓉岛 - Suggests the early onset of winter at the lotus island; 'Light cold first arrives at the lotus island.'
  • 谢池幽梦属才郎 - Alludes to a dream or romantic encounter associated with Xie Chi, a famous poet; 'The secluded dream of Xie Chi belongs to the talented young man.'
  • 几度生春草 - Symbolizes the passage of time and the cyclical nature of life; 'How many times has spring grass grown.'
  • 尘世多情易老 - Reflects the Buddhist or Taoist idea that attachment to worldly desires leads to aging and suffering; 'The world is full of emotions, and it is easy to grow old.'
  • 秋风袅袅 - Describes the gentle, melancholic nature of the autumn breeze; 'The autumn wind sways gently.'
  • 晓来羞对，香芷汀洲，枯荷池沼 - Expresses a sense of shame or regret due to the contrast between the beauty of nature and one's own emotional state; 'In the morning, I am ashamed to face the fragrant orchids on the sandbank and the withered lotus in the pond.'
  • 银锁横波，远山浅黛无心扫 - Describes a serene landscape with a silver lock (likely a bridge or reflection) crossing the waves and distant mountains lightly outlined; 'The silver lock crosses the waves, and the distant mountains are lightly outlined as if swept carelessly.'
  • 湘江人去叹无依，此意从谁表 - Suggests a sense of abandonment or loneliness, with the Xiang River being a symbol of emotional depth or sorrow; 'People have left the Xiang River, lamenting their lack of support, and this feeling cannot be expressed.'
  • 喜趁良宵月姣，况难逢，人间两好 - Expresses joy and appreciation for rare, beautiful moments; 'Happy to seize the beautiful moon on a good night, especially when it is hard to find such a perfect moment in life.'
  • 莫辞人醉，醉入屏山，只愁天晓 - Suggests indulging in pleasure while being mindful of the fleeting nature of time; 'Do not refuse to get drunk, as getting drunk will lead you into the screen-like mountains, but only worry about the coming dawn.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 芙蓉岛 - A common poetic name for a place associated with lotus flowers, often symbolizing purity or beauty; 'Lotus Island.'
  • 谢池 - Named after Xie Chi, a famous poet from the Southern Dynasties, known for his love of nature and poetry; 'Xie Chi's pond.'
  • 湘江 - A major river in Hunan Province, often associated with sorrow and longing in Chinese literature; 'Xiang River.'
  • 屏山 - A poetic term for mountains that resemble a screen or curtain, often used to describe a picturesque landscape; 'Screen-like mountains.'

Proper Nouns

  • 芙蓉岛 - A poetic name for a place associated with lotus flowers; 'Lotus Island.'
  • 谢池 - Named after the famous poet Xie Chi; 'Xie Chi's pond.'
  • 湘江 - A major river in Hunan Province; 'Xiang River.'
  • 屏山 - A poetic term for mountains that resemble a screen or curtain; 'Screen-like mountains.'

[1387]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 女蓬首喘战 - Means 'the girl had disheveled hair and was panting and trembling.'

[1388]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 旦而朱至 - Temporal marker indicating Zhu's arrival at dawn.
  • 再拜炷香 - Bowing twice and lighting incense, a sign of deep respect.
  • 求救子命 - Seeking help to save one's son's life.
  • 请假僧寺一巨镬 - Borrowing a large cauldron from a temple.
  • 煎油二十斤 - Frying twenty pounds of oil.
  • 吾当施法，摄其祟 - Performing a ritual to capture a malevolent spirit.
  • 令君阖族见之 - Making the entire family see the ritual.
  • 乃即池边焚符檄数道召将吏 - Burning talismans and summoning documents to call upon officials and soldiers.

  • 露背突兀如蓑衣 - Protruding back like a raincoat.
  • 浮游中央，回首四顾 - Floating in the center, turning its head and looking around.
  • 乃大白鳖也 - A large white turtle.
  • 若为物所鐍致 - Trapped by some object.
  • 跂曳至庭下 - Dragging itself to the courtyard.
  • 顿足呀口 - Standing on its feet and opening its mouth.
  • 犹若向人作乞命态 - Begging for its life.
  • 镬油正沸 - The oil in the cauldron was boiling.
  • 自匍匐投其中 - Crawling into the boiling oil.
  • 糜溃而死 - Melting and dying.
  • 观者骇惧流汗 - Spectators were terrified and sweating.
  • 朱戒其家，俟油冷，以斧破鳖 - Zhu warned his family to wait until the oil cooled, then break the turtle with an axe.

  • 须极干，入人参，茯苓，龙骨末成丸 - Drying them thoroughly, then mixing with ginseng, poria, and dragon bone powder to make pills.
  • 托为补药，命病者晨夕饵之 - Pretending they were tonic pills and ordering the sick person to take them morning and night.

  • 如其言，药尽而病愈 - As he said, when the medicine was finished, the illness was cured.
  • 盖尚遗种类云 - It seems there are still some left.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 朱 - Zhu, a character in the story, possibly a Taoist priest or magician.
  • 舒父母 - Shu's parents, the family seeking help.
  • 僧寺 - A Buddhist temple, where the cauldron is borrowed.
  • 符檄 - Talismans and summoning documents used in exorcism rituals.
  • 将吏 - Officials and soldiers, summoned in the ritual.
  • 白鳖 - A large white turtle, often associated with water deities or spirits in Chinese folklore.
  • 人参，茯苓，龙骨 - Ginseng, poria, and dragon bone, traditional Chinese medicinal ingredients.
  • 补药 - Tonic pills, used to treat illnesses.

Proper Nouns

  • 朱 - Zhu, the character performing the ritual.
  • 舒父母 - Shu's parents, the family seeking help.
  • 僧寺 - A Buddhist temple.
  • 符檄 - Talismans and summoning documents.
  • 将吏 - Officials and soldiers.
  • 白鳖 - A large white turtle.
  • 人参，茯苓，龙骨 - Ginseng, poria, and dragon bone, traditional Chinese medicinal ingredients.
  • 补药 - Tonic pills.

[1389]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 唐大定初 - A time reference meaning 'in the early years of the Dading era'; note: the Dading era is a reign title of the Jin dynasty, not the Tang.
  • 随新罗使 - Accompanied by a Silla envoy.
  • 风吹至一处 - Blown by the wind to a place; metaphorical or magical expression indicating sudden, unexpected travel.
  • 人皆长须 - All people had long beards; physical characteristic of the inhabitants of this strange land.
  • 语与唐言通 - Their language was understood by the Tang dialect; indicates mutual intelligibility.
  • 号长须国 - They called it the Land of the Long Beards; name given to the place by the Tang scholar.
  • 人物甚盛 - The people and customs were very flourishing; refers to the prosperity of the land.
  • 栋宇衣冠，稍异中国 - Their buildings, clothing, and hats were somewhat different from those of China; indicates a culture similar but not identical to that of China.
  • 地曰扶桑洲 - The place was called Fusang洲; Fusang is a classical Chinese term for a mythical land in the East, often associated with the sun rising.
  • 其署官品，有正长；戢波；日没；岛逻等号 - The titles of their officials included: Zheng Chang (正长), Ji Bo (戢波), Ri Mo (日没), Dao Luo (岛逻), etc.; unique titles of officials in the fictional state.
  • 士人历谒数处，其国皆敬之 - The scholar visited several places, and the country honored him; indicates the scholar's prestige.
  • 行两日，方至一大城，甲士门焉 - They traveled for two days and finally reached a large city, where armored soldiers stood at the gate; indicates the grandeur of the capital.
  • 使者导士人入伏谒 - Envoys led the scholar in to bow in homage; formal ritual of submission.
  • 殿宇高敞，仪卫如王者 - The palace was grand and open, and the ceremonial guards were like those of a king; describes the opulence of the court.
  • 乃拜士人为司风长，兼驸马 - They appointed the scholar as the Chief of the Wind, and also as a驸马 (son-in-law); symbolic and honorific title.
  • 其主甚美，有须数十根 - The queen was very beautiful, with dozens of mustaches; striking detail, indicating a gender-fluid or androgynous ruler.
  • 士人威势烜赫，富有珠玉 - The scholar had great power and was rich in pearls and jade; describes his elevated status.
  • 然每归，见其妻则不悦 - But every time he returned, seeing his wife made him unhappy; indicates emotional conflict.
  • 其王于月满夜则大会 - The king held a grand feast on the night of the full moon; recurring event with cultural significance.
  • 后遇会，士人见嫔姬悉有须，因赋诗曰 - Later, when he attended the feast, he saw that all the concubines had mustaches, so he composed a poem saying...; indicates the scholar's poetic response to the phenomenon.
  • 花无叶不妍，女有须亦丑 - A flower without leaves is not beautiful; a woman with mustaches is also ugly; poetic critique of the androgynous women.
  • 经十馀年，士人有一儿二女 - After more than ten years, the scholar had one son and two daughters; indicates the culmination of his stay in the land.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 唐大定初 - The Dading era is a reign title of the Jin dynasty, not the Tang; likely a historical error or fictional anachronism.
  • 新罗 - Silla (698–935 CE), a Korean kingdom; proper noun to be consistently translated as 'Silla'.
  • 扶桑洲 - Fusang洲 or Fusang Island, a mythical land in classical Chinese texts associated with the rising sun; often linked with the Sun God or Sun Empire.
  • 正长；戢波；日没；岛逻 - Fictional titles of officials in the fictional state; should be translated as Zheng Chang (正长), Ji Bo (戢波), Ri Mo (日没), Dao Luo (岛逻).
  • 司风长 - Chief of the Wind, a symbolic or ceremonial title; the term 司风 may relate to wind control or weather regulation.
  • 驸马 - Son-in-law, a title given to a man married to a royal princess; in this context, it is an honorific title bestowed upon the scholar.
  • 月满夜 - Full moon night, a traditional time for festivals, gatherings, and rituals in Chinese culture.

  • 人孰無死也 - Philosophical statement meaning 'who among people does not die?' It emphasizes the inevitability of death.
  • 必欲高潔以逃之 - Phrase meaning 'if one wishes to escape it by being pure and noble,' suggesting a desire to transcend mortality through virtue.

  • 死固难免 - Statement meaning 'death is indeed unavoidable,' reinforcing the inevitability of death.
  • 但当值此死耳 - Phrase meaning 'but one should only face this death,' suggesting acceptance of death as a natural part of life.
  • 朝求井上之李，暮拔园中之葵 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'seeking plums from the well in the morning and pulling up chrysanthemums from the garden in the evening,' symbolizing a life of constant striving and effort.
  • 劳苦迎合，驱驰世途 - Phrase meaning 'toil and flatter, rush about in the world,' describing a life of hardship and constant effort to fit in.
  • 忧愤迭兴，惊疑靡一 - Phrase meaning 'anxiety and anger arise repeatedly, with no moment free from shock and doubt,' describing a life of emotional turmoil.

  • 终无益也 - Statement meaning 'it will ultimately be of no benefit,' criticizing the idea of relying on miracles or supernatural events to escape death.
  • 未必得此者乎 - Rhetorical question meaning 'is it not even possible to achieve this?' It questions the feasibility of relying on miracles.
  • 托赤松以遨游，隐桔中以行乐 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'to entrust oneself to the spirit of Cang Jie (a legendary figure) to wander freely, or to hide in the orange grove to enjoy life,' suggesting a life of freedom and enjoyment.
  • 餐菊英，纫兰佩 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'to eat the petals of chrysanthemums, to wear a fragrant orchid as a pendant,' symbolizing a life of simplicity and elegance.

  • 与木石通情，猿鹤同梦 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'to communicate with wood and stone, to dream with apes and cranes,' suggesting a life of harmony with nature.
  • 虽片月浮云，不足以喻其闲，飞花流水，莫能以状其适 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'even a sliver of the moon or floating clouds are not enough to describe its leisure, nor can flying flowers and flowing water capture its ease,' describing a life of profound peace and contentment.
  • 天地至乐，斯人久享历焉 - Statement meaning 'the greatest joy in the world, this person has long enjoyed and experienced it,' praising a life of deep contentment.

  • 一木岂能支哉 - Can one tree support it? A rhetorical question emphasizing the futility of a single individual to prevent a larger disaster.
  • 子诚孰识世故者 - You truly know the ways of the world; a compliment to the speaker's wisdom and experience.
  • 方其凄风寒雨，杏褪桃残 - Poetic description of a desolate and bleak scene, with the blossoms of apricot and peach trees fading.
  • 山路箫条，愁云十里 - The mountain path is desolate, and ten miles of sorrowful clouds; vivid image of loneliness and gloom.

  • 夫谁曰不乐乎 - Who would say there is not joy? A rhetorical question affirming the presence of joy despite hardship.
  • 安排青眼，窥人多矣 - Many have cast their favoring glances, but none compare to you; '青眼' is a classical term meaning 'favoring gaze' or 'favor,' often associated with a person of high regard.
  • 不辞李下私嫌，竟赴桑间密约 - I will not hesitate to face the suspicion of being under a plum tree, and will still go to the secret appointment in the mulberry grove; metaphorical expression referencing the idiom '李下不正冠' (not to adjust one's hat under a plum tree), which warns against suspicion of impropriety.
  • 惓惓为君道也 - I am earnestly telling you this; '惓惓' conveys a sense of sincerity and earnestness.
  • 咀嚼卿言，不觉俗心顿破 - Chewing on your words, I am unaware that my worldly heart has suddenly been broken; metaphor for the transformative effect of the speaker's words, breaking the listener’s attachment to worldly concerns.
  • 但不能置此身耳 - But I cannot put this body aside; implies a conflict between the desire to transcend worldly matters and the physical reality of being bound to the body.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 赵家乔木 - Refers to the Zhao family’s noble and long-standing lineage, a common metaphor in Chinese literature for a family with deep roots and honor.
  • 李下私嫌 - References the classical idiom '李下不正冠' (not to adjust one's hat under a plum tree), which is a metaphor for avoiding suspicion of impropriety. In this context, the speaker is willing to risk this suspicion to be with the beloved.
  • 桑间密约 - Secret appointment in the mulberry grove; a poetic allusion to a clandestine or romantic meeting, possibly referencing the story of the 'Sangzhi' (mulberry grove), a common motif in Chinese poetry for love and secrecy.
  • 青眼 - Classical term for favor or affection, originally from the story of Ji Kang, who gave a 'green eye' (青眼) to those he favored and a 'white eye' (白眼) to those he disliked.
  • 俗心顿破 - Phrase that reflects the Buddhist or Daoist idea of breaking free from worldly desires and attachments; implies a spiritual or emotional awakening.

  • 赵家 - The Zhao family, a noble family in Chinese literature, though not a specific historical family unless contextually indicated.

  • 魂气升于天，形魄归于地 - Classical belief that the soul ascends to heaven, while the physical body returns to the earth.
  • 无质矣 - Literally 'without substance,' meaning that the person has no physical form left.
  • 狐狸媚物，动为人祸 - Common belief that foxes are cunning and can cause harm to people.
  • 世有功德于民 - Means 'has done good deeds for the people.'
  • 殆非其比 - Phrase meaning 'it is probably not comparable to them,' suggesting that the entity being discussed is of a higher moral standing.
  • 可闻姓氏否 - Polite way of asking 'May I ask for your name or lineage?'
  • 燧人氏之苗裔也 - 'Sui Ren Shi's descendants,' referring to the legendary figure Sui Ren Shi, one of the Three Sovereigns in Chinese mythology.
  • 始祖统丙丁，镇南方 - 'The ancestor who governed the elements of fire and metal and controlled the south,' referring to the Five Elements and the Four Cardinal Directions.
  • 复以德王神农陶唐氏 - 'Again, through virtue, he became the king of Shen Nong and Tao Tang,' referring to the legendary figures Shen Nong and Emperor Yao.
  • 后又王于西汉 - 'Later, he became king during the Western Han Dynasty.'
  • 因食采于宋 - 'Because he was granted fiefs in Song,' indicating a hereditary noble title.
  • 远祖无忌 - 'The distant ancestor named Wu Ji,' where '无忌' is a name.
  • 以威猛暴耗，人不可亲 - 'Because of his fierce and brutal nature, people could not approach him.'
  • 遂为白泽氏所执 - 'He was captured by Bai Ze,' referring to a legendary beast expert in Chinese mythology.
  • 樵童牧竖，得以知名 - 'Even woodcutters and shepherds know him,' emphasizing widespread recognition.
  • 汉明帝时，佛法东流 - 'During the reign of Emperor Ming of Han, Buddhism spread eastward.'
  • 摩腾，竺法兰二罗汉 - 'Mo Teng and Zhuo Lan, two Arhats,' referring to Buddhist monks who were among the first to introduce Buddhism to China.
  • 十四代祖 - 'The 14th generation ancestor.'
  • 令显扬释教 - 'To promote and spread the Buddhist teachings.'
  • 遂封为长明公 - 'He was then enfeoffed as the Duke Chang Ming,' indicating a high-ranking title.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 祯 - An auspicious sign or omen.
  • 鬼物 - Ghosts or spirits.
  • 魂气升于天，形魄归于地 - A traditional Chinese belief about the afterlife.
  • 狐狸媚物 - Foxes are often depicted as cunning and sometimes malevolent in Chinese folklore.
  • 燧人氏 - One of the Three Sovereigns, credited with the invention of fire.
  • 神农 - Another of the Three Sovereigns, known for agriculture and medicine.
  • 陶唐氏 - A reference to Emperor Yao, one of the Five Emperors.
  • 西汉 - The Western Han Dynasty, a period of Chinese history.
  • 宋 - A state during the Spring and Autumn period, later a kingdom during the Warring States period.
  • 白泽 - A mythical creature that knows the names and natures of all things.
  • 汉明帝 - Emperor Ming of Han, who ruled from 57 to 75 CE.
  • 佛法东流 - The introduction of Buddhism to China.
  • 摩腾，竺法兰 - Early Buddhist monks who came to China during the Han Dynasty.
  • 长明公 - A noble title, indicating a high status in the Buddhist hierarchy.

Proper Nouns

  • 祯 - Auspicious sign or omen.
  • 鬼物 - Ghosts or spirits.
  • 燧人氏 - Sui Ren Shi, a legendary figure.
  • 神农 - Shen Nong, a legendary figure.
  • 陶唐氏 - Tao Tang, a legendary figure.
  • 西汉 - Western Han Dynasty.
  • 宋 - State of Song.
  • 白泽 - Bai Ze, a mythical creature.
  • 汉明帝 - Emperor Ming of Han.
  • 摩腾 - Mo Teng, a Buddhist monk.
  • 竺法兰 - Zhuo Lan, a Buddhist monk.
  • 长明公 - Duke Chang Ming, a noble title.

[1410]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 魏武季年 - Refers to the later years of Cao Cao, known as 'Wei Wu' (魏武), a powerful warlord and statesman of the late Eastern Han period.
  • 灭佛法，诛道士 - Means 'abolished Buddhism and killed Taoist priests,' indicating a policy of religious suppression.
  • 长明公幽死 - Refers to the death of a Taoist priest named Changming Gong under mysterious circumstances.
  • 魏文嗣位 - Means 'Wei Wen succeeded to the throne,' referring to the ascension of Cao Pi, son of Cao Cao, who became the first emperor of the state of Wei.
  • 佛法重兴 - Means 'Buddhism was revived,' indicating a reversal of the previous policy.
  • 长明世子袭之 - Means 'Changming's heir inherited the position,' suggesting that Changming Gong's son took over his role.
  • 至开元初 - Refers to the early years of the Kaiyuan era during the reign of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 玄宗治骊山，起造华清宫 - Means 'Emperor Xuanzong built the Huaqing Palace on Lishan,' a famous palace known for its hot springs.
  • 作朝元阁，立长生殿 - Means 'constructed the Chaoyuan Pavilion and the Changsheng Hall,' indicating the construction of additional buildings within the palace complex.
  • 以馀财因修此寺 - Means 'used the remaining funds to repair this temple,' suggesting that some of the resources were allocated for the maintenance of a nearby temple.
  • 群像既立，遂设东幢 - Means 'after the statues were erected, the eastern pagoda was set up,' indicating the completion of a significant part of the temple.
  • 帝与妃子自汤殿宴罢，微行佛庙，礼陁伽境 - Means 'the emperor and his concubine, after a banquet at the hot spring palace, secretly visited the Buddhist temple and paid homage to the Bodhisattva Guanyin.'
  • 妃子谓帝曰：‘当于飞之秋，不当令东幢岿然无偶。’ - Means 'the concubine said to the emperor, 'It should be appropriate for this season of flying (referring to the spring season), not to leave the eastern pagoda standing alone without a pair.''
  • 帝即命立西幢 - Means 'the emperor immediately ordered the construction of the western pagoda,' indicating a response to the concubine's suggestion.
  • 遂封某为西明夫人 - Means 'he then enfeoffed someone as the Lady of the Western Brightness,' suggesting a formal recognition or title given to a person, possibly the concubine or another figure.
  • 因设珊瑚帐，固予形貌，于是巽生不复强暴矣 - Means 'thereafter, a coral canopy was set up, stabilizing the appearance, and thus the evil influence of the west no longer prevailed,' indicating a symbolic or ritual action to restore balance or harmony.
  • 歌舞丝竹 - Refers to music and dance, specifically the playing of string and wind instruments.
  • 四者孰妙 - Means 'which of the four is the most wonderful,' asking which of the four mentioned arts is the most exquisite.
  • 承先祖之明德 - Means 'inheriting the bright virtue of ancestors,' referring to the legacy of virtue passed down from forebears.
  • 禀炎上之烈性 - Means 'possessing the intense nature of fire,' suggesting a passionate or fiery temperament.
  • 动即煨山岳而烬原野，静则烛幽暗而破昏蒙 - Means 'when in motion, it can burn mountains and plains; when at rest, it illuminates darkness and dispels confusion,' describing the dual nature of the subject, which can be both destructive and enlightening.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 魏武 - Refers to Cao Cao, a prominent warlord and statesman of the late Eastern Han period, known for his military prowess and political acumen.
  • 魏文 - Refers to Cao Pi, the son of Cao Cao and the first emperor of the state of Wei.
  • 开元 - Refers to the Kaiyuan era, a period of prosperity and cultural flourishing during the reign of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 玄宗 - Refers to Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, known for his patronage of the arts and his role in the golden age of Tang poetry.
  • 骊山 - Refers to Lishan, a mountain near Xianyang, where the Huaqing Palace was located.
  • 华清宫 - Refers to the Huaqing Palace, a famous imperial retreat known for its hot springs.
  • 朝元阁 - Refers to the Chaoyuan Pavilion, a building within the Huaqing Palace.
  • 长生殿 - Refers to the Changsheng Hall, another building within the Huaqing Palace.
  • 东幢 - Refers to the eastern pagoda, a structure within the temple complex.
  • 西幢 - Refers to the western pagoda, a structure added later to complement the eastern pagoda.
  • 西明夫人 - Refers to the Lady of the Western Brightness, a title given to a person, possibly the concubine or another figure.
  • 珊瑚帐 - Refers to a canopy made of coral, a luxurious and symbolic decoration.
  • 巽生 - Refers to the direction of the southwest, associated with wind and change in the I Ching (Book of Changes).
  • 歌舞丝竹 - Refers to the traditional Chinese arts of music and dance, specifically the playing of string and wind instruments.
  • 先祖之明德 - Refers to the virtues and moral qualities inherited from ancestors.
  • 炎上之烈性 - Refers to the intense and fiery nature, often associated with the element of fire in Chinese philosophy.
  • 煨山岳而烬原野 - Refers to the destructive power of fire, capable of burning mountains and plains.
  • 烛幽暗而破昏蒙 - Refers to the illuminating power of light, capable of dispelling darkness and confusion.

Proper Nouns

  • 魏武 - Cao Cao, a prominent warlord and statesman of the late Eastern Han period.
  • 魏文 - Cao Pi, the son of Cao Cao and the first emperor of the state of Wei.
  • 开元 - The Kaiyuan era, a period of prosperity and cultural flourishing during the reign of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 玄宗 - Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, known for his patronage of the arts and his role in the golden age of Tang poetry.
  • 骊山 - Lishan, a mountain near Xianyang, where the Huaqing Palace was located.
